title: Weightliftes PLEASE list your weight so I can know how strong you are!

transcript.text: Can people please, please, please, when they film themselves working out, write exactly how much weight they're doing and how many reps they do, for the love of God, nobody does that, and it totally boggles my mind.

I mean, I feel like I'm an alien falling down to some weird, strange, fucked up planet, dude, when people are filling themselves, working out, and they don't say anything about how many reps they do and how much weight they do or if they do it.
The only people who do that are the CrossFit people, and that's awesome, but how about all that other stuff, all that other gym stuff, you know?
You get all kinds of people doing all kinds of stuff, and you never know.

You know, I open up a video, a magazine, muscle magazine, and somebody's like, you know, some fitness competitor's like, yeah, look at their routine, and they, yeah, I do this for 30 minutes, and then I do that for 30, you know, I do 10 reps, and it's like, well, how much weight?
I don't care about any of that.
All I care about is how freaking strong you are.
And also, when you film yourself, for the love of God, do, if you're going to film yourself, do show us how strong you are.
Don't, don't, don't, don't, um, half-ass it.
I got a, uh, membership at DIYMuscle.com to watch Muscle Chicks, big ones.
And, uh, let's see, SheMuscle, and her biceps, which is the biggest one, has the most videos.
And most of the time, these girls, dude, they don't, they're like curling 25-pound dumbbells in like 10 reps, and then they're like, okay, let's go, it's like, what?
I could do that when I was 13 and 95 pounds.
It's like, give me, show me what you got.
I mean, there's some girls who do some strong stuff.
Like, I saw Lindsay Cope, leg press 1100 pounds.
I saw some girl, I can't remember what her name is, uh, dumbbell press 100-pound dumbbells five times.
Um, I found, like, YouTube video of this one girl squatting, box squatting 272 times.
And then Erica Kern squatting 315-12 times after doing 225-12 times and 135-12 times.
Maybe I'll add these videos as links.
And, uh, you know, like, but you have to search this stuff out.
Yeah, it's like finding a needle in a haystack.
Everybody else is just like, you know, and some people, you know, and I'm, I'm just like, thanks for the instructional videos.
But some people are like, yeah, this is how you do this properly.
And they do, like, one rep.
And it's like, come on, show me how many you can do.
I mean, I want to know how strong people are.
I want to be able to look at somebody's body and know how strong they are and guess how strong they are and then find out how strong they are after I look.
It's like a game, you know.
It's like charades, you know.
It's fun.
